You can now store unlimited context for AI, but without structure, your memories quickly become a messy pile of information that you have no idea how to work with. We've been there too.   
So, we've been heads-down building, and Memory Studio now has a Notion-style editor: with version history, rich formatting, enhanced file understanding, markdown support and more. [...] Here's what's we shipped:

hira siddiqui
AI Context Flow

AI Context Flow

OpenAI just made AI memory automatic.   
The problem? you can no longer fully audit what it remembers about you.

Dreaming V3 (launched June 4) doesn't ask you to save memories anymore. It runs in the background, reads across years of your conversations, and synthesizes a profile of you, and automatically updating as your life changes.   
It remembered your Singapore trip was in the future. Now it knows you went. You didn't tell it that. It figured it out.

results.title: MuleRun: Raise an AI that actually learns how you work
results.content:
On the pattern-learning side, MuleRun tracks not just what you ask for, but how you work — the sequence of operations, the tools you reach for in specific contexts, the outputs you accept versus revise. Over time, it builds a working model of your decision logic, so it can begin anticipating the next step in a workflow rather than waiting for instruction.

For a developer workflow spanning terminal sessions and coding environments, the practical implication is that your agent can observe recurring patterns — say, a sequence of build, test, and deploy commands you run in a particular order — and start preparing or executing those proactively. The 24/7 runtime also means long-running processes don't get interrupted when you step away. [...] MuleRun's memory isn't a static snapshot that accumulates indefinitely without discrimination. When your behavior shifts significantly — new task types, different communication patterns, a new domain of work — the agent picks up on those signals through your actual usage. New patterns, consistently reinforced, progressively carry more weight than older ones that are no longer being activated. [...] Subscribe

MuleRun

## Raise an AI that actually learns how you work

1.4K followers

results.content:
Ratings

Ease of use

Reliability

Value for money

Customization

Report

•1 review

#### What's great

fast performance (29)customer support (16)context aware (3)meeting recording (85)ease of use (58)meeting highlights (28)AI-generated summaries (102)automatic transcription (73)integration with Google Meet (25)Ask Fathom feature (22)makes meeting life easier (6)

Fathom AI has been an absolute game changer for me. I didn’t realize how much mental energy I was losing in meetings until I started using it—it genuinely feels like I’ve gained that time and focus back. Being able to automatically capture conversations, pull clips, and revisit key moments has made a huge difference in how I show up and follow through. [...] What I really love:

 It automatically highlights important parts of the conversation so I can go back instantly instead of scrubbing through recordings
 It makes my follow-up emails faster, sharper, and more actionable
 It keeps me organized without adding another system I have to manage
 It gives me a second brain for every client conversation

The biggest win? It elevates the quality of my coaching. I’m not guessing or relying on memory—I’m working from exactly what was said, with clarity.

If you’re on calls all day and not using Fathom, you’re working harder than you need to.

This is one of those tools that quietly becomes indispensable.

#### What needs improvement [...] Based on 314 reviews

Review Fathom?

Reviewers largely see Fathom as a reliable meeting sidekick that reduces note-taking, delivers fast, accurate summaries, and makes follow-up easier with action items, searchable transcripts, clips, and CRM-friendly outputs. Many praise how quietly it joins calls, integrates with tools like Google Calendar, Zoom, Meet, and HubSpot, and works well across languages and accents. A few drawbacks come up repeatedly: the bot can feel awkward, search and team UI can be uneven, and mobile, phone, and in-person support have lagged. Founders from Slashy and DOConvert echo its usefulness for sales follow-up and cross-call querying.

Summarized with AI

results.title: Slack Competitors & Alternatives (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
A major differentiator is its built-in AI meeting capture, including summaries, notes, and action items, so decisions don’t disappear into chat history. That can make it a stronger choice than Slack when meetings are frequent and follow-through needs to be automatic.

SoWork also leans into customization and engagement with avatars and office layouts, which can boost participation and team bonding. Teams should weigh considerations like performance on older devices and how well it fits with existing project tool integrations, but for culture and presence, it’s a standout alternative.

### Best for

Ideal for remote or hybrid teams that want a virtual office with spontaneous conversations and meeting capture.

### Standout features [...] ### Best for

Best for teams that want task management and communication unified with clear visibility.

### Standout features

 ✓Chat inside each task
 ✓Task-first collaboration and coordination
 ✓Analytics dashboards for progress visibility
 ✓Simple, focused work management
 ✓Clearer accountability across the team
